subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Anthropic Principle
description: Learn The Anthropic Principle in one minute.

hook.text: The universe's fundamental constants seem perfectly tuned for life. Change one slightly and stars can't form.

buildup.text: If gravity were slightly stronger, the universe would have collapsed. Slightly weaker, no stars would ignite.

discovery.text: The anthropic principle says we observe these values because only they allow observers like us to exist.

twist.text: Critics call it circular reasoning—we exist, so of course we see life-friendly constants. It explains nothing.

climax.text: Others argue it's a powerful selection effect. In a multiverse, most universes are lifeless—we're in a rare one.

punchline.text: The universe fits life perfectly. Coincidence, design, or selection?

quiz.question: What does the anthropic principle argue?
options: We observe life-friendly constants because only such a universe allows observers to exist
The universe was intentionally designed for life by an intelligent force
Physical constants change over time to allow life to develop
